content: EHR optimization strategies address documented burdens: (1) Documentation improvement - scribes (virtual or in-person), voice recognition, template optimization reducing physician documentation time by 1-2 hours/day; (2) Inbox management - team-based routing, standing orders for common requests, limiting patient portal message volume; (3) Alert optimization - reducing alert fatigue by suppressing low-value alerts (from 50-100/day to 10-20 high-priority); (4) Interoperability advancement - TEFCA framework enabling nationwide exchange, FHIR APIs for app integration; (5) Usability improvements - specialty-specific workflows, reduced clicks for common tasks. Physicians spend 2:1 ratio of EHR time to face-to-face time (6 hours EHR for 4 hours patient care), contributing to burnout. Well-optimized EHRs show 20-30% reduction in documentation time.
